title: ochna rupununiensis
intro: Ochna rupununiensis, also known as the Ochna tree or Poison Berry tree, is a plant species in the family Ochnaceae. It is a small tree or shrub native to the Guiana Shield, a region in South America. Ochna rupununiensis has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures.
